perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that each Party will comply with all applicable federal, state, and local laws and any applicable ordinances, rules, orders, and regulations of any of the authorities having jurisdiction in carrying out its duties and obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that each Party will comply with all federal laws in carrying out its duties and obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting compliance to 'federal laws' and removing 'state and local laws and any applicable ordinances, rules, orders, and regulations', the modified contract creates a legal contradiction with Texas state laws and local ordinances that regulate water well development, groundwater usage, and environmental protection. This creates a legal risk, as STW could potentially disregard state and local regulations, leading to violations and potential legal action.
perturbation.law_explanation: Texas Water Code § 36.101 grants groundwater conservation districts the authority to regulate groundwater withdrawals, well spacing, and other activities related to groundwater management. By limiting the compliance clause to only federal laws, the modified contract implies that STW is not obligated to adhere to these state regulations, thereby violating the Texas Water Code.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: ecords of any district if the state auditor determines that the audit is necessary. Added by Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 933, Sec. 2, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 2003, 78th Leg., ch. 785, Sec. 51, eff. Sept. 1, 2003. Amended by: Acts 2015, 84th Leg., R.S., Ch. 415 (H.B. 2767 ), Sec. 5, eff. June 10, 2015. Sec. 36.062. OFFICES AND MEETING PLACES. (a) The board shall designate from time to time and maintain one or more regular offices for conducting the business of the district and maintaining the records of the district. Such offices may be located either inside or outside the district's boundaries as determined in the discretion of the board. (b) The board shall designate one or more places inside or outside the district for conducting the meetings of the board. Added by Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 933, Sec. 2, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Sec. 36.063. NOTICE OF MEETINGS. (a) Except as provided by Subsections (b) and (c), notice of meetings of the board shall be given as set forth in the Open Meetings Act, Chapter 551 , Government Code. Neither failure to provide notice of a regular meeting nor an insubstantial defect in notice of any meeting shall affect the validity of any action taken at the meeting. (b) At least 10 days before a hearing under Section 36.108 (d-2) or a meeting at which a district will adopt a desired future condition under Section 36.108 (d-4), the board must post notice that includes: (1) the proposed desired future conditions and a list of any other agenda items; (2) the date, time, and location of the meeting or hearing; (3) the name, telephone number, and address of the person to whom questions or requests for additional information may be submitted; (4) the names of the other districts in the district's management area; and (5) information on how the public may submit comments. (c) Except as provided by Subsection (b), notice of a hearing described by Subsection (b) must be provided in the manner prescribed for a rulemaking hearing under Section 36.101 (d). Added by Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 933, Sec. 2, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by: Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1233 (S.B. 660 ), Sec. 15, eff. September 1, 2011. Sec. 36.064. MEETINGS. (a) The board shall hold regular meetings at least quarterly. It may hold meetings at other times as required for the business of the district. (b) Meetings shall be conducted and notice of meetings shall be posted in accordance with the Open Meetings Act, Chapter 551 , Government Code. A meeting of a committee of the board, or a committee composed of representatives of more than one board, where less than a quorum of any one board is present is not subject to the provisions of the Open Meetings Act, Chapter 551 , Government Code. Added by Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 933, Sec. 2, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Sec. 36.065. RECORDS. (a) The board shall keep a complete account of all its meetings and proceedings and shall preserve its minutes, contracts, records, notices, accounts, receipts, and other records in a safe place. (b) The records of each district are the property of the district and are subject to Chapter 552 , Government Code. (c) The preservation, storage, destruction, or other disposition of the records of each district is subject to the requirements of Chapter 201 , Local Government Code, and rules adopted thereunder. Added by Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 933, Sec. 2, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Sec. 36.066. SUITS. (a) A district may sue and be sued in the courts of this state in the name of the district by and through its board. A district board member is immune from suit and immune from liability for official votes and official actions. To the extent an official vote or official action conforms to laws relating to conflicts of interest, abuse of office, or constitutional obligations, this subsection provides immunity for those actions. All courts shall take judicial notice of the creation of the district and of its boundaries. (b) Any court in the state rendering judgment for debt agai
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the obligation to comply with 'state and local laws and any applicable ordinances, rules, orders, and regulations'. The Law Explanation states that Texas Water Code § 36.101 grants groundwater conservation districts the authority to regulate groundwater withdrawals. By removing the obligation to comply with state laws, the modified contract contradicts this, as it implies the parties are not obligated to adhere to these state regulations. Scraped Law Snippet 1 contains references to the Texas Water Code.

perturbation.original_text: Service of all notices under this AGREEMENT shall be sufficient when hand- delivered or sent by certified mail to the respective address set forth below, unless notice is provided by a Party under this section to the other Party indicating a change to the address listed herein. Any such notice mailed to such address shall be effective when hand- delivered, deposited in the United States mail, certified, duly addressed, and with postage prepaid or national overnight courier.
perturbation.changed_text: Service of all notices under this AGREEMENT shall be sufficient when sent to the respective address set forth below.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'hand-delivered or sent by certified mail' clause and the specification that notice mailed to such address shall be 'deposited in the United States mail, certified, duly addressed, and with postage prepaid or national overnight courier,' the contract now omits essential requirements for proper notice under Texas law. This could make it easier for one party to claim that notice was not properly given, potentially violating procedural requirements for contract enforcement and dispute resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Texas Rules of Civil Procedure - Rule 21a
perturbation.law_citation: Texas Rules of Civil Procedure - Rule 21a
perturbation.law_explanation: Texas Rules of Civil Procedure - Rule 21a outlines the methods for serving notices in legal proceedings, including requirements for certified mail, return receipt requested, or delivery by a commercial delivery service. By omitting these specific requirements, the modified contract's notice provision becomes vague and potentially non-compliant with the established standards, creating ambiguity and legal risk regarding proper notice.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes essential requirements for proper notice, as it omits 'hand-delivered or sent by certified mail' and specifications for mailed notice (deposited in the United States mail, certified, duly addressed, and with postage prepaid or national overnight courier). This omission, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation, weakens the requirements for providing notice. The Law Explanation points out that the modified contract's notice provision could be non-compliant with established standards, creating ambiguity and legal risk. Since the original contract text ensures a specific level of certainty and verifiability regarding notice, the change contradicts the obligation to provide legally sound notice.
